pattern: Rotating a block only ever reorders its own elements, so a block length only works if every block's slice of the final sorted array is reachable from that same block purely by rotation -- a condition a doubled-block substring check settles in one pass per block.
hints: Rotation can never move an element out of its own block, so the multiset of values sitting in each block of length k must already match the multiset that belongs there in the fully sorted array.
Beyond the multiset matching, the exact order matters too: the sorted array's slice for that block must be some cyclic rotation of the original block, not just any rearrangement.
To check whether one sequence is a cyclic rotation of another quickly, concatenate the original block with itself and look for the target slice as a contiguous run inside that doubled sequence.

edge_inputs.description: already sorted array, every divisor works
strictly decreasing array, no divisor works except when forced trivially
a block rotation would fix one block length but not a larger one
prime length, only divisors are 1 and n itself
highly composite length with several candidate divisors
one block already sorted while another needs a full rotation

stress.description: large random array with a highly composite length
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    composite_lengths = [720, 840, 60480, 100000]
    n = min([c for c in composite_lengths if c >= 1], key=lambda c: abs(c - max(1, size)))
    nums = [rng.randint(1, 100000) for _ in range(n)]
    return [nums]
